name: Absorb
effect: Drains HP: the user recovers half of the damage dealt as HP (1.3x recovery if holding a Big Root).

name: Acid
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by 1 stage.

name: Acid Armor
effect: Raises the user's Defense by 2 stages.

name: Acid Spray
effect: Has a 100% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by 2 stages.

name: Acrobatics
effect: Power doubles to 110 if the user is not holding an item.

name: Acupressure
effect: Raises a random stat of the user or an adjacent ally by 2 stages, skipping stats already at their max stage. Fails if no stat can be raised.

name: Aerial Ace
effect: Never misses; this move does not check accuracy.

name: Aeroblast
effect: Has an increased critical hit ratio. Only learnable by Lugia.

name: After You
effect: Makes the target move immediately after the user this turn, regardless of priority or Speed. Fails if the target would already move next, or has already moved.

name: Agility
effect: Raises the user's Speed by 2 stages.

name: Air Cutter
effect: Has an increased critical hit ratio. Hits all adjacent opponents.

name: Air Slash
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Ally Switch
effect: The user swaps positions with its ally. The chance of success drops the more times it's used consecutively (resets if it fails or another move is used).

name: Amnesia
effect: Raises the user's Special Defense by 2 stages.

name: AncientPower
effect: Has a 10% chance to raise the user's Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense, and Speed by one stage each.

name: Aqua Jet
effect: Has priority +1, letting the user move first in its priority bracket. No additional effect.

name: Aqua Ring
effect: The user heals 1/16 of its max HP at the end of each turn while this is active (1.3x if holding a Big Root). The effect carries over if the user is switched out via Baton Pass.

name: Aqua Tail
effect: No additional effect.

name: Arm Thrust
effect: Hits 2-5 times in one turn (35% chance of 2-3 hits, 15% chance of 4-5 hits; always 5 hits with Skill Link, 4-5 with Loaded Dice).

name: Aromatherapy
effect: Cures every Pokemon in the user's party of its non-volatile status condition (active Pokemon with Sap Sipper other than the user itself are not cured).

name: Assist
effect: Uses a random move known by another Pokemon in the user's party.

name: Assurance
effect: Power doubles if the target has already taken damage this turn from something other than Belly Drum, confusion, Curse, or Pain Split.

name: Astonish
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Attack Order
effect: Has an increased critical hit ratio. Only learnable by Vespiquen.

name: Attract
effect: Causes the target to become infatuated, making it unable to attack about 50% of the time. Fails if the user and target are the same gender, either is genderless, or the target is already infatuated.

name: Aura Sphere
effect: Never misses; this move does not check accuracy.

name: Aurora Beam
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Attack by 1 stage.

name: Autotomize
effect: Raises the user's Speed by 2 stages and reduces the user's weight by 100 kg for as long as it remains active.

name: Avalanche
effect: Power doubles if the user was hit by the target this turn. Has priority -4, so it usually moves last.

name: Barrage
effect: Hits 2-5 times in one turn (35% chance of 2-3 hits, 15% chance of 4-5 hits).

name: Barrier
effect: Raises the user's Defense by 2 stages.

name: Baton Pass
effect: Switches the user out for another party member, transferring the user's stat stage changes and effects like confusion or Aqua Ring to the replacement.

name: Beat Up
effect: Hits once for the user and once for each unfainted, non-statused party member; each hit's power is based on that Pokemon's base Attack stat.

name: Belly Drum
effect: Raises the user's Attack by 12 stages (maxing it out) in exchange for losing half of its maximum HP, rounded down.

name: Bestow
effect: The target receives the user's held item. Fails if the user has no item or the target already holds one.

name: Bide
effect: The user endures attacks for two turns (moving with priority +1), then unleashes double the total damage it took during that time on whoever hit it last.

name: Bind
effect: Traps the target, preventing it from switching out, and deals damage at the end of each turn for four or five turns.

name: Bite
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Blast Burn
effect: The user must recharge on the following turn (unable to act) if this move is successful.

name: Blaze Kick
effect: Has an increased critical hit ratio and a 10% chance to burn the target.

name: Blizzard
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Has a 10% chance to freeze the target. Never misses while Snow (Hail) is active.

name: Block
effect: Prevents the target from switching out.

name: Blue Flare
effect: Has a 20% chance to burn the target. Only learnable by Reshiram.

name: Body Slam
effect: Has a 30% chance to paralyze the target. Deals double damage to a target that used Minimize.

name: Bolt Strike
effect: Has a 20% chance to paralyze the target. Only learnable by Zekrom.

name: Bone Club
effect: Has a 10% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Bone Rush
effect: Hits 2-5 times in one turn (35% chance of 2-3 hits, 15% chance of 4-5 hits).

name: Bonemerang
effect: Hits twice in one turn.

name: Bounce
effect: The user springs up on the first turn and attacks on the second. Has a 30% chance to paralyze the target.

name: Brave Bird
effect: The user takes recoil damage equal to 33% of the damage dealt to the target.

name: Brick Break
effect: If it hits, removes the effects of Reflect, Light Screen, and Aurora Veil from the target's side of the field before dealing damage.

name: Brine
effect: Power doubles if the target's HP is at or below half of its maximum.

name: Bubble
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Speed by 1 stage.

name: BubbleBeam
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Speed by 1 stage.

name: Bug Bite
effect: If successful, steals the target's held Berry and eats it immediately.

name: Bug Buzz
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by 1 stage.

name: Bulk Up
effect: Raises the user's Attack and Defense by 1 stage each.

name: Bulldoze
effect: Hits all adjacent Pokemon. Has a 100% chance to lower each target's Speed by 1 stage.

name: Bullet Punch
effect: Has priority +1, letting the user usually move first. No additional effect.

name: Bullet Seed
effect: Hits 2-5 times in one turn (35% chance of 2-3 hits, 15% chance of 4-5 hits).

name: Calm Mind
effect: Raises the user's Special Attack and Special Defense by 1 stage each.

name: Camouflage
effect: Changes the user's type based on the current battle terrain.

name: Captivate
effect: Lowers the Special Attack of all adjacent opposing Pokemon by 2 stages. Has no effect on targets of the same gender as the user (or genderless targets).

name: Charge
effect: Raises the user's Special Defense by 1 stage, and doubles the power of the user's next Electric-type move.

name: Charge Beam
effect: Has a 70% chance to raise the user's Special Attack by 1 stage.

name: Charm
effect: Lowers the target's Attack by 2 stages.

name: Chatter
effect: Has a 100% chance to confuse the target. Only learnable by Chatot.

name: Chip Away
effect: Ignores the target's stat stage changes, including evasiveness, when calculating the hit and damage.

name: Circle Throw
effect: Has priority -6 (moves nearly last). Forces the target to switch out for a random party member if both Pokemon are still standing after the hit.

name: Clamp
effect: Traps the target, preventing it from switching out, and deals damage at the end of each turn for four or five turns.

name: Clear Smog
effect: Resets all of the target's stat stages back to 0.

name: Close Combat
effect: Lowers the user's Defense and Special Defense by 1 stage each.

name: Coil
effect: Raises the user's Attack, Defense, and accuracy by 1 stage each.

name: Comet Punch
effect: Hits 2-5 times in one turn (35% chance of 2-3 hits, 15% chance of 4-5 hits).

name: Confuse Ray
effect: Causes the target to become confused.

name: Confusion
effect: Has a 10% chance to confuse the target.

name: Constrict
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Speed by 1 stage.

name: Conversion
effect: Changes the user's type to match the type of the move in its first move slot.

name: Conversion 2
effect: Changes the user's type to one that resists or is immune to the last move used against it by the target.

name: Copycat
effect: Uses the last move used by any Pokemon (including itself) in the battle.

name: Cosmic Power
effect: Raises the user's Defense and Special Defense by 1 stage each.

name: Cotton Guard
effect: Raises the user's Defense by 3 stages.

name: Cotton Spore
effect: Lowers the Speed of all adjacent opposing Pokemon by 2 stages.

name: Counter
effect: Has priority -5. Deals double the damage the user took from the last physical move that hit it this turn, to whoever hit it.

name: Covet
effect: If successful and the user isn't holding an item, steals the target's held item.

name: Crabhammer
effect: Has an increased critical hit ratio.

name: Cross Chop
effect: Has an increased critical hit ratio.

name: Cross Poison
effect: Has an increased critical hit ratio and a 10% chance to poison the target.

name: Crunch
effect: Has a 20% chance to lower the target's Defense by 1 stage.

name: Crush Claw
effect: Has a 50% chance to lower the target's Defense by 1 stage.

name: Crush Grip
effect: Power scales with the target's current HP — roughly 120 * (current HP / max HP), so it hits hardest against full-HP targets. Only learnable by Regigigas.

name: Curse
effect: If the user is not Ghost-type, lowers its own Speed by 1 stage while raising its Attack and Defense by 1 stage each. If the user is Ghost-type, it instead loses half its max HP to curse the target, causing the target to lose 1/4 of its max HP at the end of each turn.

name: Cut
effect: No additional effect.

name: Dark Pulse
effect: Has a 20% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Dark Void
effect: Puts all adjacent opposing Pokemon to sleep. Effectively only usable by Darkrai.

name: Defend Order
effect: Raises the user's Defense and Special Defense by 1 stage each.

name: Defense Curl
effect: Raises the user's Defense by 1 stage. Doubles the power of the user's Ice Ball and Rollout while active.

name: Defog
effect: Lowers the target's evasiveness by 1 stage and clears entry hazards and screens from both sides of the field.

name: Destiny Bond
effect: If the user faints from a hit before its next move, the attacker that knocked it out faints as well.

name: Detect
effect: Has priority +4. Protects the user from most attacks this turn.

name: Dig
effect: The user burrows underground on the first turn (dodging most attacks) and strikes on the second turn.

name: Disable
effect: Disables the target's last-used move for 4 turns, preventing it from being selected.

name: Discharge
effect: Hits all adjacent Pokemon. Has a 30% chance to paralyze each target.

name: Dive
effect: The user dives underwater on the first turn (dodging most attacks) and strikes on the second turn.

name: Dizzy Punch
effect: Has a 20% chance to confuse the target.

name: Doom Desire
effect: Deals damage two turns after being used, hitting whoever is in that position at the time. Only learnable by Jirachi.

name: Double Hit
effect: Hits twice in one turn.

name: Double Kick
effect: Hits twice in one turn.

name: Double Team
effect: Raises the user's evasiveness by 1 stage.

name: Double-Edge
effect: The user takes recoil damage equal to 33% of the damage dealt to the target.

name: DoubleSlap
effect: No effect text is provided on the PokeMMO Wiki page beyond the base attack data.

name: Draco Meteor
effect: Lowers the user's Special Attack by 2 stages.

name: Dragon Claw
effect: No additional effect.

name: Dragon Dance
effect: Raises the user's Attack and Speed by 1 stage each.

name: Dragon Pulse
effect: No additional effect.

name: Dragon Rage
effect: Always deals exactly 40 HP of damage to the target, regardless of level or stats.

name: Dragon Rush
effect: Has a 20% chance to make the target flinch. Damage doubles and it never misses against a target that used Minimize.

name: Dragon Tail
effect: Has priority -6. Forces the target to switch out for a random ally if both Pokemon are still standing after the hit (fails against Ingrain users, Suction Cups holders, or if it only hit a substitute).

name: DragonBreath
effect: No effect text is provided on the PokeMMO Wiki page beyond the base attack data.

name: Drain Punch
effect: Drains HP: the user recovers half of the damage dealt as HP (1.3x recovery if holding a Big Root).

name: Dream Eater
effect: Only works if the target is asleep. The user recovers half of the damage dealt as HP (1.3x recovery if holding a Big Root).

name: Drill Peck
effect: No additional effect.

name: Drill Run
effect: Has an increased critical hit ratio.

name: Dual Chop
effect: Hits twice in one turn.

name: DynamicPunch
effect: No effect text is provided on the PokeMMO Wiki page beyond the base attack data.

name: Earth Power
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by 1 stage.

name: Earthquake
effect: Damage doubles if the target is using Dig.

name: Echoed Voice
effect: For every consecutive turn this move is used by at least one Pokemon, its power is multiplied by the number of turns passed, up to a maximum multiplier of 5.

name: Egg Bomb
effect: No additional effect.

name: Electro Ball
effect: Power depends on (user's current Speed / target's current Speed) rounded down: 150 if the ratio is 4 or more, 120 if 3, 80 if 2, 60 if 1, 40 if less than 1. If the target's current Speed is 0, power is 40.

name: Electroweb
effect: Has a 100% chance to lower the target's Speed by 1 stage.

name: Embargo
effect: For 5 turns, the target's held item has no effect (forme-change effects are unaffected, but other item effects are negated). The target also cannot use Fling or Natural Gift during this time.

name: Ember
effect: Has a 10% chance to burn the target.

name: Encore
effect: For its next 3 turns, the target is forced to repeat its last move used. Ends early if that move runs out of PP. Fails if the target is already encored, hasn't moved yet, has 0 PP for that move, or the move is one of several exempted moves (e.g. Assist, Copycat, Me First, Metronome, Mimic, Mirror Move, Sketch, Sleep Talk, Struggle, Transform).

name: Endeavor
effect: Deals damage equal to (target's current HP - user's current HP). Has no effect if the target's current HP is already less than or equal to the user's.

name: Endure
effect: The user survives any attack this turn with at least 1 HP. Has a 1/X chance of succeeding, where X starts at 1 and triples each consecutive successful use; the count resets if the move fails or the user's prior move wasn't a protection move. Fails if the user moves last that turn.

name: Energy Ball
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by 1 stage.

name: Entrainment
effect: Changes the target's Ability to match the user's. Fails against many specific abilities (e.g. As One, Battle Bond, Comatose, Disguise, Imposter, Trace, Wonder Guard, and others), or if both Pokemon already share the same ability.

name: Eruption
effect: Power is equal to (user's current HP * 150 / user's maximum HP), rounded down, with a minimum of 1.

name: Explosion
effect: The user faints after using this move, even if it fails due to no target. Cannot be used if any active Pokemon has the Damp Ability.

name: Extrasensory
effect: Has a 10% chance to make the target flinch.

name: ExtremeSpeed
effect: The PokeMMO Wiki page for this move has no effect/description text populated (confirmed). No secondary effect beyond high priority (+2) damage.

name: Facade
effect: Power doubles if the user is burned, paralyzed, or poisoned. The physical-damage-halving effect from the user's own burn is ignored.

name: Faint Attack
effect: This move bypasses accuracy checks (never misses). The PokeMMO Wiki page has no effect/description text populated beyond this.

name: Fake Out
effect: Has a 100% chance to make the target flinch. Fails unless it is the user's first turn on the field.

name: Fake Tears
effect: Lowers the target's Special Defense by 2 stages.

name: False Swipe
effect: Leaves the target with at least 1 HP, never causing it to faint.

name: FeatherDance
effect: Sharply lowers the target's Attack stat.

name: Feint
effect: If successful, breaks through the target's Baneful Bunker, Detect, King's Shield, Protect, or Spiky Shield for the turn, allowing other Pokemon to attack it normally. Also breaks side-wide protections like Crafty Shield, Mat Block, Quick Guard, or Wide Guard for the turn.

name: Fiery Dance
effect: Has a 50% chance to raise the user's Special Attack by 1 stage.

name: Final Gambit
effect: Deals damage equal to the user's current HP. If successful, the user faints.

name: Fire Blast
effect: Has a 10% chance to burn the target.

name: Fire Fang
effect: Has a 10% chance to burn the target and a separate 10% chance to make it flinch.

name: Fire Pledge
effect: If combined with an ally's Grass Pledge on the same turn, becomes a 150-power combo move that creates a sea of fire on the target's side for 4 turns. If combined with an ally's Water Pledge, creates a rainbow on the user's side for 4 turns that doubles secondary effect chances.

name: Fire Punch
effect: Has a 10% chance to burn the target.

name: Fire Spin
effect: Traps the target for 4-5 turns, preventing it from switching, and deals damage equal to 1/8 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn. Ends early if either Pokemon leaves the field or the target uses Rapid Spin/Substitute.

name: Fissure
effect: Deals damage equal to the target's maximum HP (OHKO). Actual accuracy is (user's level - target's level + 30)%. Fails if the target is a higher level than the user.

name: Flail
effect: Power increases the less HP the user has left, up to a maximum of 200 power at very low HP.

name: Flame Burst
effect: If successful, the target's ally also loses 1/16 of its maximum HP, rounded down, unless it has the Magic Guard Ability.

name: Flame Charge
effect: Has a 100% chance to raise the user's Speed by 1 stage.

name: Flame Wheel
effect: Has a 10% chance to burn the target. Also thaws the user if it is frozen.

name: Flamethrower
effect: Has a 10% chance to burn the target.

name: Flare Blitz
effect: Has a 10% chance to burn the target. The user takes recoil damage equal to 33% of the HP lost by the target. Also thaws the user if it is frozen.

name: Flash
effect: Lowers the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Flash Cannon
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by 1 stage.

name: Flatter
effect: Raises the target's Special Attack by 1 stage and confuses it.

name: Fling
effect: Power depends on the user's held item. The item is consumed and, for certain items, its normal effect (e.g. a berry's effect) also triggers on the target.

name: Fly
effect: Charges on the first turn (during which the user evades most attacks) and strikes on the second turn.

name: Focus Blast
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by 1 stage.

name: Focus Energy
effect: Raises the user's critical hit ratio by 2 stages.

name: Focus Punch
effect: The user loses focus and does nothing if it is hit by a damaging attack before it can execute this move.

name: Follow Me
effect: Until the end of the turn, all single-target attacks from opposing Pokemon are redirected to the user.

name: Force Palm
effect: Has a 30% chance to paralyze the target.

name: Foresight
effect: As long as the target remains active, its evasiveness stat changes are ignored, and Normal- and Fighting-type moves can hit it even if it is a Ghost type.

name: Foul Play
effect: Damage is calculated using the target's Attack stat (including its stat stage changes) instead of the user's.

name: Frenzy Plant
effect: If successful, the user must recharge on the following turn and cannot select a move.

name: Frost Breath
effect: Always results in a critical hit unless the target is protected against critical hits (e.g. by Shell Armor).

name: Frustration
effect: Power increases the lower the user's Happiness is, up to a maximum of 102 power.

name: Fury Attack
effect: Hits the target two to five times in a row.

name: Fury Cutter
effect: Power doubles with each consecutive successful hit, up to a maximum of 160 power. Resets if the move misses or a different move is used.

name: Fury Swipes
effect: Hits the target two to five times in a row. If a hit breaks the target's Substitute, remaining hits still deal damage.

name: Fusion Bolt
effect: Power doubles if the last move used by any Pokemon this turn was Fusion Flare.

name: Fusion Flare
effect: Power doubles if the last move used by any Pokemon this turn was Fusion Bolt.

name: Future Sight
effect: Deals damage two turns after use, hitting whoever occupies the target's original position at that time. Not affected by Protect/Detect or other move-blocking effects when it lands.

name: Gastro Acid
effect: Suppresses the target's Ability, rendering it ineffective as long as the target remains active.

name: Gear Grind
effect: Hits twice in one turn. If the first hit breaks the target's Substitute, the second hit still deals damage.

name: Giga Drain
effect: The user recovers HP equal to half the damage dealt to the target, rounded half up.

name: Giga Impact
effect: If successful, the user must recharge on the following turn and cannot select a move.

name: Glaciate
effect: Has a 100% chance to lower the target's Speed by 1 stage.

name: Glare
effect: Paralyzes the target.

name: Grass Knot
effect: Power is based on the target's weight, up to a maximum of 120 power for very heavy targets.

name: Grass Pledge
effect: If combined with an ally's Water Pledge on the same turn, creates a swamp on the target's side for 4 turns that quarters their Speed.

name: GrassWhistle
effect: The PokeMMO Wiki page has no effect/description text populated for this move (confirmed). Based on its accuracy and category, it functions as a sleep-inducing status move.

name: Gravity
effect: For 5 turns, grounds all Pokemon (letting Ground-type moves hit Flying types), reduces evasiveness, and prevents moves that require flying/bouncing/high in the air.

name: Growl
effect: Lowers the Attack of all adjacent opponents by 1 stage.

name: Growth
effect: Raises the user's Attack and Special Attack by 1 stage each; boosted to 2 stages each in harsh sunlight.

name: Grudge
effect: Until the user's next turn, if an opposing move knocks out the user, that move loses all its remaining PP.

name: Guard Split
effect: The user and the target both have their Defense and Special Defense set to the average of their combined stats.

name: Guard Swap
effect: The user swaps its Defense and Special Defense stat stage changes with the target's.

name: Guillotine
effect: The target faints instantly (OHKO) if this attack hits.

name: Gunk Shot
effect: Has a 30% chance to poison the target.

name: Gust
effect: Power doubles if the target is using Bounce, Fly, or Sky Drop.

name: Gyro Ball
effect: Power increases the slower the user is relative to the target, up to a maximum of 150 power.

name: Hammer Arm
effect: Lowers the user's own Speed by 1 stage.

name: Harden
effect: Raises the user's Defense by 1 stage.

name: Haze
effect: Resets the stat stage changes of all active Pokemon on the field to 0.

name: Head Charge
effect: The user takes recoil damage equal to 1/4 of the HP lost by the target.

name: Head Smash
effect: The user takes recoil damage equal to 1/2 of the HP lost by the target.

name: Headbutt
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Heal Bell
effect: Cures every Pokemon in the user's party of its non-volatile status condition.

name: Heal Block
effect: For 5 turns, prevents the target from restoring any HP via healing moves or items.

name: Heal Order
effect: The user restores half of its maximum HP.

name: Heal Pulse
effect: Restores half of the target's maximum HP (boosted to 3/4 if the user has the Mega Launcher Ability).

name: Healing Wish
effect: The user faints; the Pokemon that replaces it has its HP fully restored and any status condition cured.

name: Heart Stamp
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Heart Swap
effect: The user swaps all of its stat stage changes with the target's.

name: Heat Crash
effect: Power is based on the user's weight compared to the target's — a heavier user deals more damage. Power doubles against a target that has used Minimize.

name: Heat Wave
effect: Has a 10% chance to burn the target.

name: Heavy Slam
effect: Power is based on the user's weight compared to the target's — a heavier user deals more damage. Power doubles against a target that has used Minimize.

name: Helping Hand
effect: Multiplies the power of an adjacent ally's attack this turn by 1.5.

name: Hex
effect: Power doubles if the target has a non-volatile status condition (e.g. burn, poison, paralysis, sleep, freeze).

name: Hi Jump Kick
effect: The PokeMMO Wiki page has no effect/description text populated for this move (confirmed).

name: Hidden Power
effect: The PokeMMO Wiki page has no effect/description text populated for this move (confirmed).

name: Hone Claws
effect: Raises the user's Attack and accuracy by 1 stage each.

name: Horn Attack
effect: The PokeMMO Wiki page has no effect/description text populated for this move (confirmed).

name: Horn Drill
effect: The target faints instantly (OHKO) if this attack hits. Fails if the target is a higher level than the user.

name: Horn Leech
effect: The user recovers HP equal to half the damage dealt to the target.

name: Howl
effect: Raises the Attack of the user and all allies by 1 stage.

name: Hurricane
effect: Has a 30% chance to confuse the target. Can hit targets that are in the air (e.g. using Fly/Bounce). Never misses in rain; only 50% accurate in harsh sunlight.

name: Hydro Cannon
effect: If successful, the user must recharge on the following turn and cannot select a move.

name: Hydro Pump
effect: The PokeMMO Wiki page has no effect/description text populated for this move (confirmed) — a high-power Water-type attack with no secondary effect noted.

name: Hyper Beam
effect: If successful, the user must recharge on the following turn and cannot select a move.

name: Hyper Fang
effect: Has a 10% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Hyper Voice
effect: No additional effect.

name: Hypnosis
effect: Causes the target to fall asleep.

name: Ice Ball
effect: If successful, locks the user into this move for up to 5 turns (or until it misses or can't be used). Power doubles with each consecutive successful hit, and doubles again if the user previously used Defense Curl. If called via Sleep Talk, only lasts one turn.

name: Ice Beam
effect: Has a 10% chance to freeze the target.

name: Ice Fang
effect: Has a 10% chance to freeze the target and a separate 10% chance to make it flinch.

name: Ice Punch
effect: Has a 10% chance to freeze the target.

name: Ice Shard
effect: No additional effect. Has priority +1.

name: Icicle Crash
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Icicle Spear
effect: Hits the target two to five times in a row (35% chance of 2-3 hits, 15% chance of 4-5 hits). Always hits 5 times if the user has the Skill Link Ability, or 4-5 times if holding Loaded Dice.

name: Icy Wind
effect: Has a 100% chance to lower the Speed of all adjacent opponents by 1 stage.

name: Imprison
effect: Prevents all opposing Pokemon from using any move that the user also knows, for as long as the user remains active.

name: Incinerate
effect: Destroys the target's held item if it is a Berry or a Gem (does not affect Pokemon with the Sticky Hold Ability). The lost item cannot be recovered with Recycle or the Harvest Ability.

name: Inferno
effect: Has a 100% chance to burn the target.

name: Ingrain
effect: The user restores 1/16 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn, but cannot switch out (and cannot be forced out either) unless via Baton Pass or a similar effect. Also removes the user's immunity to Ground-type moves and entry hazards for the duration.

name: Iron Defense
effect: Sharply raises the user's Defense by 2 stages.

name: Iron Head
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Iron Tail
effect: Has a 30% chance to lower the target's Defense by 1 stage.

name: Judgment
effect: This move's type changes to match the user's held Plate item.

name: Jump Kick
effect: If this attack misses, the user takes crash damage equal to half its maximum HP, rounded down. Pokemon with the Magic Guard Ability are unaffected by crash damage.

name: Karate Chop
effect: Has a higher-than-normal critical hit ratio.

name: Kinesis
effect: Lowers the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Knock Off
effect: If the target is holding a removable item (ignoring Sticky Hold), power is multiplied by 1.5 and the target loses that item (unless the user faints from the attack). Cannot remove items from Pokemon with Sticky Hold or certain special legendary items (Orbs, Plates, Drives, Memories, Rusted weapons). Lost items cannot be recovered via Recycle or Harvest.

name: Last Resort
effect: Fails unless the user knows this move plus at least one other move, and has already used every other move it knows at least once since becoming active (or since Transforming).

name: Lava Plume
effect: Has a 30% chance to burn the target.

name: Leaf Blade
effect: Has a higher-than-normal critical hit ratio.

name: Leaf Storm
effect: Sharply lowers the user's own Special Attack by 2 stages.

name: Leaf Tornado
effect: Has a 50% chance to lower the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Leech Life
effect: The user recovers HP equal to half the damage dealt to the target, rounded half up (1.3x if the user holds Big Root, rounded half down).

name: Leech Seed
effect: Saps 1/8 of the target's maximum HP at the end of each turn (1.3x if the recipient holds Big Root), transferring it to the user's position. Ends if the target switches out or successfully uses Rapid Spin/Mortal Spin. Grass-type Pokemon are immune to being seeded.

name: Leer
effect: Lowers the Defense of all adjacent opponents by 1 stage.

name: Lick
effect: Has a 30% chance to paralyze the target.

name: Light Screen
effect: For 5 turns (8 if the user holds Light Clay), halves damage the user's side takes from special attacks (0.66x in double battles). Critical hits ignore this effect. Removed early if the side is hit by Brick Break, Psychic Fangs, or Defog.

name: Lock-On
effect: Guarantees the user's next move against the target will hit, even during a two-turn move. Ends if either Pokemon leaves the field.

name: Lovely Kiss
effect: Causes the target to fall asleep.

name: Low Kick
effect: Power is based on the target's weight: 20 if under 10kg, 40 if under 25kg, 60 if under 50kg, 80 if under 100kg, 100 if under 200kg, 120 if 200kg or more.

name: Low Sweep
effect: Has a 100% chance to lower the target's Speed by 1 stage.

name: Lucky Chant
effect: For 5 turns, the user's side cannot be struck by a critical hit.

name: Lunar Dance
effect: The user faints; the Pokemon that replaces it has its HP and PP fully restored and any status condition cured. Fails if the user is the last unfainted team member.

name: Luster Purge
effect: Has a 50% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by 1 stage.

name: Mach Punch
effect: No additional effect. Has priority +1.

name: Magic Room
effect: For 5 turns, held items of all active Pokemon have no effect (forme-change effects are unaffected, but other item effects are negated); Fling and Natural Gift cannot be used during this time. Using this move again while active ends the effect early.

name: Magical Leaf
effect: This move bypasses accuracy checks (never misses).

name: Magma Storm
effect: Traps the target for 4-5 turns (7 if the user holds Grip Claw), dealing damage equal to 1/8 of its maximum HP (1/6 if it holds Binding Band) at the end of each turn. Ends if either Pokemon leaves the field or the target uses Rapid Spin/Mortal Spin/Substitute.

name: Magnet Bomb
effect: This move bypasses accuracy checks (never misses).

name: Magnet Rise
effect: For 5 turns, makes the user immune to Ground-type moves and to Spikes, Toxic Spikes, Sticky Web, and the Arena Trap Ability, as long as it stays active.

name: Magnitude
effect: Random power each use: 10 or 150 (5% each), 30 or 110 (10% each), 50 or 90 (20% each), 70 (30%). Damage doubles if the target is using Dig.

name: Me First
effect: If the user moves before the target, it uses the move the target chose this turn (at 1.5x power) instead of its own move. Fails against certain moves (e.g. Counter, Focus Punch, Mirror Coat, Struggle, Thief) or if the target has already moved.

name: Mean Look
effect: Prevents the target from switching out, unless it holds Shed Shell or uses an escape move like Baton Pass or U-turn. Ends if either Pokemon leaves the field.

name: Meditate
effect: Raises the user's Attack by 1 stage.

name: Mega Drain
effect: The user recovers HP equal to half the damage dealt to the target (1.3x if it holds Big Root).

name: Mega Kick
effect: No additional effect.

name: Mega Punch
effect: No additional effect.

name: Megahorn
effect: No additional effect.

name: Memento
effect: Sharply lowers the target's Attack and Special Attack by 2 stages each. The user faints immediately after, unless the move misses or there is no valid target.

name: Metal Burst
effect: Deals damage to the last opposing Pokemon that hit the user with an attack this turn, equal to 1.5x the HP lost from that attack (or 1 HP if none was lost). Fails if the user was not hit this turn.

name: Metal Claw
effect: Has a 10% chance to raise the user's own Attack by 1 stage.

name: Metal Sound
effect: Sharply lowers the target's Special Defense by 2 stages.

name: Meteor Mash
effect: Has a 20% chance to raise the user's own Attack by 1 stage.

name: Milk Drink
effect: The user restores half of its maximum HP.

name: Mimic
effect: While the user remains active, this move is replaced by the target's last used move, with full PP for that move. Fails if the target hasn't acted, the user already knows the move, or the target's move is one that cannot be copied (e.g. Metronome, Sketch, Sleep Talk, Struggle, Transform).

name: Mind Reader
effect: Guarantees the user's next move against the target will hit, even during a two-turn move. Ends if either Pokemon leaves the field.

name: Minimize
effect: Sharply raises the user's evasiveness by 2 stages. While active, certain moves (Body Slam, Dragon Rush, Flying Press, Heat Crash, Heavy Slam, Stomp, Steamroller, and similar) always hit the user and deal double damage.

name: Miracle Eye
effect: As long as the target remains active, its positive evasiveness is ignored during accuracy checks, and Psychic-type moves can hit it even if it is a Dark type.

name: Mirror Coat
effect: Deals damage to the last opposing Pokemon that hit the user with a special attack this turn, equal to twice the HP lost from that attack (or 1 HP if none was lost). Fails if the user was not hit by a special attack this turn. Priority -5.

name: Mirror Shot
effect: Has a 30% chance to lower the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Mist
effect: For 5 turns, protects the user's side from having its stat stages lowered by opposing Pokemon.

name: Mist Ball
effect: Has a 50% chance to lower the target's Special Attack by 1 stage.

name: Moonlight
effect: The user restores HP: half its maximum HP in clear weather, 2/3 in harsh sunlight, or 1/4 in rain, sandstorm, or hail/snow.

name: Morning Sun
effect: The user restores HP: half its maximum HP in clear weather, 2/3 in harsh sunlight, or 1/4 in rain, sandstorm, or hail/snow.

name: Mud Bomb
effect: Has a 30% chance to lower the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Mud Shot
effect: Has a 100% chance to lower the target's Speed by 1 stage.

name: Mud Sport
effect: For 5 turns, multiplies the power of all Electric-type moves used by any active Pokemon by 0.33.

name: Mud-Slap
effect: Has a 100% chance to lower the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Muddy Water
effect: Has a 30% chance to lower the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Nasty Plot
effect: Sharply raises the user's Special Attack by 2 stages.

name: Natural Gift
effect: Type and power depend on the user's held Berry, which is consumed. Fails if the user has no Berry, has the Klutz Ability, or is under Embargo/Magic Room.

name: Nature Power
effect: Calls a different move depending on the current terrain: Tri Attack on normal terrain, Thunderbolt on Electric Terrain, Moonblast on Misty Terrain, Energy Ball on Grassy Terrain, Psychic on Psychic Terrain.

name: Needle Arm
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Night Daze
effect: Has a 40% chance to lower the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Night Shade
effect: Deals damage equal to the user's level.

name: Night Slash
effect: Has a higher-than-normal critical hit ratio.

name: Nightmare
effect: Causes the target to lose 1/4 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn, but only while it remains asleep. Has no effect on a target that isn't asleep, and stops once it wakes up.

name: Octazooka
effect: Has a 50% chance to lower the target's accuracy by 1 stage.

name: Odor Sleuth
effect: As long as the target remains active, its positive evasiveness is ignored during accuracy checks, and Normal- and Fighting-type moves can hit it even if it is a Ghost type.

name: Ominous Wind
effect: Has a 10% chance to raise the user's Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense, and Speed by 1 stage each.

name: Outrage
effect: Locks the user in for 2-3 turns against a random opponent each turn, then confuses the user after the last turn (if not already confused).

name: Overheat
effect: Sharply lowers the user's own Special Attack by 2 stages.

name: Pain Split
effect: The user's and target's current HP are both set to the average of the two, rounded down (capped at each Pokemon's own max HP).

name: Pay Day
effect: Scatters coins that are collected after the battle, equal to the target's level x 10. Only effective once per battle.

name: Payback
effect: Power doubles if the user moves after the target this turn.

name: Peck
effect: No additional effect.

name: Perish Song
effect: Every active Pokemon that doesn't already have a perish count gets set to 4. At the end of each turn the count drops by 1 for all affected Pokemon, and any that reach 0 faint. Switching out removes the count; using Baton Pass passes it to the replacement.

name: Petal Dance
effect: Locks the user in for 2-3 turns against a random opponent each turn, then confuses the user afterward.

name: Pin Missile
effect: Hits the target two to five times in a row.

name: Pluck
effect: If the target holds a Berry, the user eats it and gains its effect for itself.

name: Poison Fang
effect: Has a 50% chance to badly poison the target.

name: Poison Gas
effect: Poisons all adjacent opponents.

name: Poison Jab
effect: Has a 30% chance to poison the target.

name: Poison Sting
effect: Has a 30% chance to poison the target.

name: Poison Tail
effect: Has a higher-than-normal critical hit ratio and a 10% chance to poison the target.

name: PoisonPowder
effect: Poisons the target. Does not make contact.

name: Pound
effect: The target is physically pounded with a long tail, a foreleg, or the like. No additional effect.

name: Powder Snow
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Has a 10% chance to freeze the target.

name: Power Gem
effect: Inflicts damage with no additional effect.

name: Power Split
effect: Adds the user's and target's Attack and Sp. Atk stats together, then splits the total equally between both.

name: Power Swap
effect: Swaps any stat changes to Attack and Sp. Atk with the target.

name: Power Trick
effect: Swaps the user's base Attack stat with its base Defense stat.

name: Power Whip
effect: Inflicts heavy damage with no additional effect.

name: Present
effect: Inflicts random damage, or heals the target by 25% of its max HP instead.

name: Protect
effect: Protects the user from all attacks this turn. May fail if used in succession.

name: Psybeam
effect: Has a 10% chance to confuse the target.

name: Psych Up
effect: Copies the target's stat changes onto the user.

name: Psychic (move)
effect: Has a 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by one stage.

name: Psycho Boost
effect: Lowers the user's Special Attack by two stages after attacking.

name: Psycho Cut
effect: High critical-hit ratio. Does not make contact.

name: Psycho Shift
effect: Transfers the user's status condition to the target.

name: Psyshock
effect: Deals damage calculated against the target's physical Defense stat instead of Sp. Def.

name: Psystrike
effect: Deals damage calculated against the target's physical Defense stat instead of Sp. Def.

name: Psywave
effect: Inflicts random damage that varies based on the user's level.

name: Punishment
effect: Power increases the more the target's stats have been boosted.

name: Pursuit
effect: Power doubles if used on a target that is switching out.

name: Quash
effect: Forces the target to act last this turn, regardless of its move's priority.

name: Quick Attack
effect: Priority attack that always strikes first (when not outsped by another priority move).

name: Quick Guard
effect: Protects the user's side of the field from priority moves this turn.

name: Quiver Dance
effect: Raises the user's Special Attack, Special Defense, and Speed by one stage each.

name: Rage
effect: The user's Attack rises each time it takes damage while this move is in use.

name: Rage Powder
effect: Draws the attention of opposing Pokemon to the user, forcing them to target it.

name: Rain Dance
effect: Summons rain for 5 turns, boosting Water-type moves and weakening Fire-type moves.

name: Rapid Spin
effect: Frees the user from binding moves and hazards (Spikes, etc.) and raises the user's Speed by one stage.

name: Razor Leaf
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. High critical-hit ratio.

name: Razor Shell
effect: Has a 50% chance to lower the target's Defense by one stage.

name: Razor Wind
effect: Charges on the first turn and strikes on the second. High critical-hit ratio.

name: Recover
effect: Restores 50% of the user's max HP.

name: Recycle
effect: Allows the user to reuse an already-consumed held item.

name: Reflect
effect: Reduces physical damage taken by the user's side for 5 turns.

name: Reflect Type
effect: The user takes on the target's type(s).

name: Refresh
effect: Cures the user's own burn, paralysis, or poison.

name: Relic Song
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Has a 10% chance to put the target to sleep. Also changes Meloetta's form.

name: Rest
effect: The user sleeps for 2 turns, fully restoring HP and curing any status condition.

name: Retaliate
effect: Power doubles if an ally fainted on the previous turn.

name: Return
effect: Power increases with the user's friendship level; higher friendship means higher power.

name: Revenge
effect: Power doubles if the user took damage from the target this turn before moving.

name: Reversal
effect: The lower the user's remaining HP, the higher this move's power.

name: Roar
effect: Forces the target to switch out for a random party member.

name: Roar of Time
effect: The user must recharge and cannot move on the next turn.

name: Rock Blast
effect: Strikes 2 to 5 times in a row.

name: Rock Climb
effect: Has a 20% chance to confuse the target. Outside of battle, lets the Pokemon scale rocky walls (HM in official games; PokeMMO usage may differ).

name: Rock Polish
effect: Raises the user's Speed by two stages.

name: Rock Slide
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Rock Smash
effect: Has a 50% chance to lower the target's Defense by one stage.

name: Rock Throw
effect: Throws a rock to inflict damage with no additional effect.

name: Rock Tomb
effect: Lowers the target's Speed by one stage.

name: Rock Wrecker
effect: The user must recharge and cannot move on the next turn.

name: Role Play
effect: The user copies the target's Ability.

name: Rolling Kick
effect: Has a 30% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Rollout
effect: Attacks over 5 turns, doubling in power with each successful hit.

name: Roost
effect: Restores 50% of the user's max HP. The user loses its Flying type until the end of the turn.

name: Round
effect: Power increases if other Pokemon on the field use Round in the same turn, in succession.

name: Sacred Fire
effect: Has a 50% chance to burn the target. Also thaws out the user if it is frozen.

name: Sacred Sword
effect: Ignores the target's stat changes to Defense and Evasion.

name: Safeguard
effect: Protects the user's side of the field from status conditions for 5 turns.

name: Sand Tomb
effect: Traps the target and deals damage for 4 to 5 turns.

name: Sand-Attack
effect: Lowers the target's accuracy by one stage. Works even against Pokémon immune to Ground.
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/Sand-Attack), extraido 2026-07-10 — efecto: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Bulbapedia, https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Sand_Attack_(move)), sin verificar contra PokeMMO

name: Sandstorm
effect: Summons a sandstorm for 5 turns. Classified as a Wind move, absorbed by the Wind Rider ability.

name: Scald
effect: Has a 30% chance to burn the target. Also thaws out the user if it is frozen.

name: Scary Face
effect: Lowers the target's Speed by two stages.

name: Scratch
effect: Inflicts damage with no additional effect.

name: Screech
effect: Harshly lowers the target's Defense by two stages.

name: Searing Shot
effect: Hits all adjacent Pokemon. Has a 30% chance to burn each target.

name: Secret Power
effect: Has an additional effect that changes depending on the terrain the battle takes place in.

name: Secret Sword
effect: Deals damage calculated against the target's physical Defense stat instead of Sp. Def.

name: Seed Bomb
effect: Fires an explosive seed at the target to inflict damage with no additional effect.

name: Seed Flare
effect: Has a 40% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by two stages.

name: Seismic Toss
effect: Inflicts damage equal to the user's level.

name: Selfdestruct
effect: Deals heavy damage and causes the user to faint. Does not halve the target's Defense (Gen 5 behavior).
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/Selfdestruct), extraido 2026-07-10 — efecto: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Bulbapedia, https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Self-Destruct_(move)), sin verificar contra PokeMMO

name: Shadow Ball
effect: Has a 20% chance to lower the target's Special Defense by one stage.

name: Shadow Claw
effect: High critical-hit ratio.

name: Shadow Force
effect: The user disappears on the first turn and strikes on the second. Ignores the target's Protect/Detect.

name: Shadow Punch
effect: This attack never misses.

name: Shadow Sneak
effect: Priority attack that always strikes first (when not outsped by another priority move).

name: Sharpen
effect: Raises the user's Attack by one stage.

name: Sheer Cold
effect: Knocks out the target in one hit if it lands. Less accurate when used by a non-Ice type.

name: Shell Smash
effect: Lowers the user's Defense and Special Defense by one stage each, but sharply raises Attack, Special Attack, and Speed by two stages each.

name: Shift Gear
effect: Raises the user's Attack by one stage and Speed by two stages.

name: Shock Wave
effect: This attack never misses.

name: Silver Wind
effect: Has a 10% chance to raise all of the user's stats by one stage.

name: Simple Beam
effect: Replaces the target's Ability with Simple.

name: Sing
effect: Puts the target to sleep with a lullaby.

name: Sketch
effect: Permanently copies the last move used by the target.

name: Skill Swap
effect: The user and the target swap Abilities.

name: Skull Bash
effect: Raises the user's Defense on the first turn, then strikes on the second turn.

name: Sky Attack
effect: Charges on the first turn and strikes on the second. High critical-hit ratio, may make the target flinch.

name: Sky Drop
effect: Takes the target into the air on the first turn and drops it to the ground, dealing damage, on the second turn.

name: Sky Uppercut
effect: Can hit Pokemon that are in the semi-invulnerable phase of moves like Fly or Bounce.

name: Slack Off
effect: Restores half of the user's max HP.

name: Slam
effect: Inflicts damage with no additional effect.

name: Slash
effect: High critical-hit ratio.

name: Sleep Powder
effect: Scatters a cloud of soporific powder that puts the target to sleep.

name: Sleep Talk
effect: Randomly uses one of the user's other moves while it is asleep.

name: Sludge
effect: Has a chance to poison the target.

name: Sludge Bomb
effect: Has a chance to poison the target.

name: Sludge Wave
effect: Hits all adjacent Pokemon. Has a chance to poison each target.

name: Smack Down
effect: Knocks flying or otherwise airborne targets down to the ground, removing their immunity to Ground-type moves.

name: SmellingSalt
effect: Deals double damage against a paralyzed target, but cures the target's paralysis.
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/SmellingSalt), extraido 2026-07-10 — efecto: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Bulbapedia, https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Smelling_Salts_(move)), sin verificar contra PokeMMO

name: Smog
effect: Exhales a toxic gas that may poison the target.

name: SmokeScreen
effect: Lowers the target's accuracy by one stage.
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/SmokeScreen), extraido 2026-07-10 — efecto: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Bulbapedia, https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Smokescreen_(move)), sin verificar contra PokeMMO

name: Snarl
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Lowers their Special Attack by one stage.

name: Snatch
effect: Steals the effect of the next healing or status move used by any other Pokemon this turn.

name: Snore
effect: Can only be used while the user is asleep. May make the target flinch.

name: Snowscape
effect: Summons a snowstorm for 5 turns, boosting the Defense of Ice-type Pokemon.

name: Soak
effect: Changes the target's type to pure Water.

name: Softboiled
effect: Restores 50% of the user's maximum HP, rounded up.
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/Softboiled), extraido 2026-07-10 — efecto: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Bulbapedia, https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Soft-Boiled_(move)), sin verificar contra PokeMMO

name: SolarBeam
effect: Charges on the first turn, attacks on the second. Skips the charge turn in harsh sunlight; power is halved in rain, sandstorm and hail.
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/SolarBeam), extraido 2026-07-10 — efecto: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Bulbapedia, https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Solar_Beam_(move)), sin verificar contra PokeMMO

name: SonicBoom
effect: Deals a fixed 20 HP of damage. Cannot hit Ghost-type Pokémon.

name: Spacial Rend
effect: High critical-hit ratio.

name: Spark
effect: An electrically charged tackle that may paralyze the target.

name: Spider Web
effect: Ensnares the target in a sticky web, preventing it from fleeing or switching out.

name: Spike Cannon
effect: Fires a volley of sharp spikes at the target 2 to 5 times in a row.

name: Spikes
effect: Scatters spikes on the opposing side of the field that damage Pokemon switching in.

name: Spit Up
effect: Releases energy stored by Stockpile as an attack; power scales with the amount of Stockpile stacks.

name: Spite
effect: Reduces the PP of the target's last used move by 4.

name: Splash
effect: The user just flops and splashes around. Nothing happens.

name: Spore
effect: Scatters spores that guarantee the target falls asleep.

name: Stealth Rock
effect: Lays floating stones that damage opposing Pokemon when they switch into battle.

name: Steamroller
effect: Has a chance to make the target flinch.

name: Steel Wing
effect: Strikes the target with stiff wings. Has a chance to raise the user's Defense by one stage.

name: Stockpile
effect: Charges up energy, raising the user's Defense and Special Defense; can be used up to 3 times, storing power for Spit Up/Swallow.

name: Stomp
effect: Stomps on the target with a huge foot. Has a chance to make it flinch.

name: Stone Edge
effect: Strikes with sharpened stones. High critical-hit ratio.

name: Stored Power
effect: Power increases with each of the user's positive stat stage boosts.

name: Storm Throw
effect: Always results in a critical hit.

name: Strength
effect: Inflicts damage with no additional effect.

name: String Shot
effect: Shoots a silk thread that harshly lowers the target's Speed by two stages.

name: Struggle Bug
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Lowers their Special Attack by one stage.

name: Stun Spore
effect: Scatters spores that paralyze the target.

name: Submission
effect: The user charges recklessly at the target, taking recoil damage equal to a quarter of the damage dealt.

name: Substitute
effect: The user sacrifices a quarter of its max HP to create a substitute that takes hits in its place.

name: Sucker Punch
effect: Priority attack that only succeeds if the target is about to use a damaging move.

name: Sunny Day
effect: Makes the sunlight harsh for 5 turns, boosting Fire-type moves and weakening Water-type moves.

name: Super Fang
effect: Cuts the target's HP in half.

name: Superpower
effect: A very powerful attack, but it lowers the user's Attack and Defense by one stage each.

name: Supersonic
effect: Emits strange sound waves that confuse the target.

name: Surf
effect: Hits all adjacent Pokemon with a huge wave that swamps the battlefield.

name: Swagger
effect: Confuses the target, but also sharply raises its Attack by two stages.

name: Swallow
effect: Absorbs energy stored by Stockpile to restore the user's HP; more Stockpile stacks means more HP restored.

name: Sweet Kiss
effect: Gives the target a sweet kiss that confuses it.

name: Sweet Scent
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents. Harshly lowers their Evasion by two stages.

name: Swift
effect: Hits all adjacent opponents with star-shaped rays that never miss.

name: Switcheroo
effect: The user swaps its held item with the target's held item.

name: Swords Dance
effect: A fighting dance that sharply raises the user's Attack by two stages.

name: Synchronoise
effect: A mysterious shockwave that only damages Pokemon sharing a type with the user.

name: Synthesis
effect: Restores the user's HP; the amount healed depends on the current weather.

name: Tail Glow
effect: The user flashes its tail to drastically raise its Special Attack by three stages.

name: Tail Slap
effect: Slaps the target with its tail 2 to 5 times in a row.

name: Tail Whip
effect: Wags its tail to lower the target's Defense by one stage.

name: Tailwind
effect: Creates a tailwind that doubles the Speed of the user's side of the field for 4 turns.

name: Take Down
effect: A reckless charge that also damages the user with recoil.

name: Taunt
effect: Taunts the target, preventing it from using status moves for 3 turns.

name: Signal Beam
effect: Has a 10% chance to confuse the target.

name: Techno Blast
effect: An attack whose type changes depending on the Drive item held by the user.

name: Teeter Dance
effect: A frenetic dance that confuses all adjacent Pokemon.

name: Telekinesis
effect: Makes the target float in the air for 3 turns, making it much easier to hit.

name: Teleport
effect: Allows the user to flee from a wild battle instantly, or switch out in a trainer battle.

name: Thief
effect: A fast attack that also steals the target's held item.

name: Thrash
effect: The user rampages for 2 to 3 turns, then becomes confused.

name: Thunder
effect: A massive lightning bolt strikes the target. Has a chance to paralyze it.

name: Thunder Fang
effect: Bites the target with electrified fangs. May paralyze it or cause it to flinch.

name: Thunder Wave
effect: Sends a weak electric shock that paralyzes the target.

name: ThunderPunch
effect: Deals damage with a 10% chance to paralyze the target.

name: ThunderShock
effect: Deals damage with a 10% chance to paralyze the target.
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/ThunderShock), extraido 2026-07-10 — efecto: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Bulbapedia, https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Thunder_Shock_(move)), sin verificar contra PokeMMO

name: Thunderbolt
effect: A strong electric discharge strikes the target. Has a chance to paralyze it.

name: Tickle
effect: Tickles the target, lowering its Attack and Defense by one stage each.

name: Torment
effect: Torments the target, preventing it from using the same move twice in a row.

name: Toxic
effect: Badly poisons the target; the damage taken increases each turn.

name: Toxic Spikes
effect: Lays poison spikes on the opposing side of the field that poison Pokemon switching in.

name: Transform
effect: The user takes on the target's form and copies all of its moves.

name: Tri Attack
effect: Fires three types of energy at the target. May burn, paralyze, or freeze it.

name: Trick
effect: The user swaps its held item with the target's held item.

name: Trick Room
effect: For 5 turns, reverses the turn order so slower Pokemon move before faster ones.

name: Triple Kick
effect: Hits up to three times, with power increasing on each successive hit (10/20/30). Accuracy is checked separately for each hit.

name: Trump Card
effect: Power is higher the fewer PP the move itself has remaining.

name: Twineedle
effect: Hits twice in one turn; each hit has a chance to poison the target.

name: Twister
effect: Has a 20% chance to make the target flinch. Power doubles against a target in a semi-invulnerable state like Fly or Bounce.

name: U-turn
effect: If the user does not faint, it switches out immediately for a selected party member after dealing damage.

name: Uproar
effect: The user attacks a random opponent for 3 turns; while active, all Pokemon wake up and no Pokemon can fall asleep.

name: V-create
effect: Lowers the user's Speed, Defense, and Special Defense by one stage each.

name: Vacuum Wave
effect: Priority attack that always strikes first (when not outsped by another priority move). No additional effect.

name: Venoshock
effect: Power doubles if the target is already poisoned.

name: ViceGrip
effect: Deals damage with no additional effect.
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/ViceGrip), extraido 2026-07-10 — efecto: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Bulbapedia, https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Vise_Grip_(move)), sin verificar contra PokeMMO

name: Vine Whip
effect: Inflicts damage with no additional effect.

name: Vital Throw
effect: This move never misses, but the user moves last this turn regardless of its Speed.

name: Volt Switch
effect: If the user does not faint, it switches out immediately for a selected party member after dealing damage.

name: Volt Tackle
effect: Has a 10% chance to paralyze the target. The user takes recoil damage equal to about a third of the damage dealt.

name: Wake-Up Slap
effect: Power doubles if the target is asleep. If the user has not fainted, the target also wakes up.

name: Water Gun
effect: Inflicts damage with no additional effect.

name: Water Pledge
effect: When used together with an ally's Fire Pledge or Grass Pledge in the same turn, creates a combined field effect (rainbow or swamp).

name: Water Pulse
effect: Has a 20% chance to confuse the target.

name: Water Sport
effect: For 5 turns, weakens all Fire-type moves used by any Pokemon on the field.

name: Water Spout
effect: Power scales with the user's current HP; strongest at full HP, weaker as HP drops.

name: Waterfall
effect: Has a 20% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Weather Ball
effect: Power doubles and the move's type changes to match the current weather (Ice in snow, Water in rain, Rock in sandstorm, Fire in sun).

name: Whirlpool
effect: Traps the target, preventing it from switching out, and deals damage over several turns.

name: Whirlwind
effect: Forces the target to switch out for a random uninjured ally.

name: Wide Guard
effect: Protects the user's entire side of the field from moves that target all adjacent Pokemon this turn.

name: Wild Charge
effect: The user takes recoil damage equal to about a quarter of the damage dealt.

name: Will-O-Wisp
effect: Burns the target.

name: Wing Attack
effect: Inflicts damage with no additional effect.

name: Wish
effect: At the end of the next turn, restores half of the user's max HP to whichever Pokemon is in the user's position at that time.

name: Withdraw
effect: Raises the user's Defense by one stage.

name: Wonder Room
effect: For 5 turns, swaps the Defense and Special Defense stats of all active Pokemon.

name: Wood Hammer
effect: The user takes recoil damage equal to about a third of the damage dealt.

name: Work Up
effect: Raises the user's Attack and Special Attack by one stage each.

name: Worry Seed
effect: Changes the target's Ability to Insomnia, unless the target has certain Abilities that cannot be replaced this way.

name: Wrap
effect: Traps the target, preventing it from switching out, and deals damage over several turns.

name: X-Scissor
effect: Deals damage without any additional effect.

name: Zap Cannon
effect: Deals damage and always paralyzes the target if the move successfully hits.

name: Zen Headbutt
effect: Deals damage and has a 20% chance to make the target flinch.

name: Wring Out
effect: Power scales with the target's remaining HP: power = 120 * (target's current HP / target's max HP), rounded half down, minimum 1.

name: Yawn
effect: Causes the target to fall asleep at the end of the next turn. Fails if the target cannot fall asleep or already has a status condition; the effect persists even if the target wakes up during the delay.

name: Magic Coat
effect: Bounces back many non-damaging moves used against the Pokémon, redirecting them at whoever used them instead of taking effect normally. Has increased priority (+4), so it usually goes before other moves in the turn. (sin verificar contra PokeMMO Wiki (página rota/inaccesible) — dato de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, por instrucción de Ferran)
source: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 — PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/Magic_Coat) devuelve error de plantilla Lua, extraido 2026-07-10

name: Metronome
effect: Calls out one random move from nearly the entire move list, then uses it. A short list of moves (Metronome itself, signature/restricted moves, and a few others) can never be called. (sin verificar contra PokeMMO Wiki (página rota/inaccesible) — dato de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, por instrucción de Ferran)
source: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 — PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/Metronome) devuelve error de plantilla Lua, extraido 2026-07-10

name: Tackle
effect: Deals damage with no additional effect. (sin verificar contra PokeMMO Wiki (página rota/inaccesible) — dato de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, por instrucción de Ferran — power/accuracy shown are the Gen 5 Black/White values specifically; Tackle's official power changed across generations, Gen 5 is used here since PokeMMO's mechanics are Gen-5-based.)
source: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 (Black/White) — PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/Tackle) no cargó tras 10 intentos, extraido 2026-07-10

name: Mirror Move
effect: The user uses the last move used by the target. The copied move is used against that target, if possible. Fails if the target has not made a move, or if the last move used cannot be copied by this move. (PP sin verificar contra PokeMMO Wiki (recuadro de datos roto por error de plantilla Lua en esa página) — dato de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, por instrucción de Ferran; Type y el resto del efecto SÍ vienen confirmados de la propia PokeMMO Wiki pese al error.)
source: PokeMMO Wiki (https://pokemmo.shoutwiki.com/wiki/Mirror_Move) confirma Type/efecto pero devuelve error de plantilla Lua para el recuadro de datos; PP de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, extraido 2026-07-10
